findings.evidence: `rg -n "\b(change|show)\b"` reports line 68 `rw [show ... = ∅ by` and line 79 `change ((idealsWithAbsNormNatLe F (N + 1)).ncard : ℝ) ≤ ...`.
findings.fix: Factor the zero-norm set equality and the nat-to-real set/cardinality coercion into small private lemmas, or rewrite the proof so the intermediate `have` is stated directly over `idealsWithAbsNormNatLe`; if a `show`/`change` remains unavoidable, add a comment explaining the required definitional equality.
findings.issue: `ncard_ideal_absNorm_le_nat` uses undocumented `show` at line 68 and `change` at line 79 to reshape set/cardinality goals by definitional equality, obscuring why these coercion/set forms match.

summary: The proofs are short, but `ncard_ideal_absNorm_le_nat` relies on undocumented `show`/`change` steps to force set/cardinality goals through private abbreviations. That is exactly the kind of brittle definitional-equality manipulation this rubric asks to remove or document.
